vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1486: feat(flywheel): auto-pickup and UAT-required toggles, persisted user-machine level

plan.title: feat(flywheel): auto-pickup and UAT-required toggles, persisted user-machine level
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: roles/flywheel.md hardcodes two human-checkpoint behaviors that should be config-driven: (1) the orchestrator inventories only work already in motion (backlog excluded); (2) the forbidden list bars merging without human UAT. v1.0 posture cannot be measured against today's safe defaults without forking the role prompt. Per operator feedback, 'auto-merge enabled' and 'UAT not required' are the same decision viewed from two angles — we collapse to two boolean toggles that together define the orchestrator's autonomy posture.
narratives.Proposal: Two boolean keys in the existing app_settings SQLite table: flywheel.auto_pickup_backlog (default false) and flywheel.require_uat_before_merge (default true). Plumb both into FlywheelLifecycleOptions and render them in the brief's configSection so roles/flywheel.md can read them conditionally. When require_uat_before_merge=false, a server-side scanner watches for PRs with all gates green and no blocker labels, schedules them with a fixed 5-minute wall-clock cooldown (TTS-announced), and after the cooldown invokes the existing dashboard merge code path. Cancel via `pan merge cancel <id>` CLI or a dashboard banner button. Failures convert into 'investigate' suggestions on the next FlywheelStatus tick. Two checkboxes on FlywheelPage drive the toggles via a new /api/flywheel/config route, mirroring the deacon/pause pattern. `pan flywheel config --get/--set` provides CLI parity.

autoDecisions.summary: Auto-merge mechanism placed server-side (POST /api/flywheel/auto-merge/schedule + persistent SQLite table) rather than orchestrator-side in-memory.
autoDecisions.rationale: Persistence across dashboard restart, cancel path that works regardless of orchestrator session state, single source of truth for the dashboard cancel banner, and avoids reinventing the merge serializer. The orchestrator role still owns the decision to schedule; the server owns the cooldown clock and execution.

autoDecisions.summary: Cooldown stored as absolute ISO timestamp (scheduledMergeAt) compared against Date.now().
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body specifies 'sleep/wake does not reset the timer (it counts wall-clock, not active time)'. Storing a delta would drift across system sleep; absolute timestamps survive sleep cleanly.

autoDecisions.summary: pending_auto_merges has UNIQUE(issueId) WHERE status IN ('pending','merging').
autoDecisions.rationale: Avoids duplicate cooldown timers when the orchestrator schedules the same PR twice (e.g. on tick-loop restart). Schedule endpoint returns 200 with the existing entry rather than 409 — re-scheduling is idempotent and benign.

autoDecisions.summary: Cancel during 'merging' state returns 409; cancel during 'pending' returns 200.
autoDecisions.rationale: Once the executor has atomically transitioned to 'merging', the merge code path has begun (rebase + push to work agent); cancelling at that point is racy and dangerous. The cooldown window IS the cancellation window.

autoDecisions.summary: Auto-merge scanner re-checks isAutoMergeEligible(issueId) immediately before transitioning to 'merging'.
autoDecisions.rationale: PR state can flip during the 5-minute window (CI restarts, blocker label added). Re-checking guards against surprising merges of newly-ineligible PRs.

autoDecisions.summary: Blocker label list is needs-design, needs-discussion, do-not-merge (verbatim from issue body).
autoDecisions.rationale: These labels already exist in the workflow (roles/flywheel.md excludes the first two from inventory). do-not-merge is the conventional auto-merge stopper. Encoded as a single exported constant so the predicate, the role prompt, and any future surface use one canonical list.

autoDecisions.summary: Toggle UI lives on FlywheelPage header (two checkboxes), not in SettingsPage.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body explicitly says 'Two checkboxes on FlywheelPage'. The flywheel page is where operators already engage with run lifecycle; the toggles are part of the run's autonomy posture.

autoDecisions.summary: Skill ↔ CLI updates land in the same bead as the CLI change.
autoDecisions.rationale: scripts/lint-skills.sh enforces drift detection; landing them separately would break CI between commits.

autoDecisions.summary: Existing dashboard MERGE button code path is the single auto-merge execution primitive.
autoDecisions.rationale: It already handles PR-state pre-checks (OPEN, checks not failing, not already merged), serialization (per-project), and post-merge lifecycle. Auto-merge calls into the same internal function the human button uses, so behavior parity is automatic.

autoDecisions.summary: TTS rate limiting: one announcement per (issueId, state-transition).
autoDecisions.rationale: Prevents a 'scheduled' announcement firing again on dashboard restart for the same pending entry, and prevents 5 simultaneously-eligible PRs from producing 5 back-to-back announcements within seconds.

items.title: Add flywheel.auto_pickup_backlog and flywheel.require_uat_before_merge keys to app-settings.ts

narrative.Action: Extend src/lib/database/app-settings.ts with two new exported constants (FLYWHEEL_AUTO_PICKUP_BACKLOG_KEY, FLYWHEEL_REQUIRE_UAT_BEFORE_MERGE_KEY) and four functions (isFlywheelAutoPickupBacklog, setFlywheelAutoPickupBacklog, isFlywheelRequireUatBeforeMerge, setFlywheelRequireUatBeforeMerge), mirroring the existing isFlywheelGloballyPaused/setFlywheelGloballyPaused shape. Defaults on missing-key or DatabaseError: false for auto_pickup_backlog, true for require_uat_before_merge.

subItems.title: FLYWHEEL_AUTO_PICKUP_BACKLOG_KEY = 'flywheel.auto_pickup_backlog' is exported; getter returns false on a fresh DB and after explicit set false; returns true after set true.

FLYWHEEL_REQUIRE_UAT_BEFORE_MERGE_KEY = 'flywheel.require_uat_before_merge' is exported; getter returns true on a fresh DB (preserves today's behavior) and after explicit set true; returns false after set false.
Both getters route through Effect.runSync(getSettingProgram(...).pipe(catchTag('DatabaseError', () => Effect.succeed(<default>)))), matching the existing isFlywheelGloballyPaused pattern.

Vitest unit tests in src/lib/database/__tests__/app-settings.test.ts cover: default-on-empty-db, round-trip set/get for both booleans, both keys, and that the two keys are independent (setting one does not affect the other).
npm run typecheck and npm test pass.

items.title: Plumb the two toggle values into FlywheelLifecycleOptions and render in the brief configSection

narrative.Action: Add autoPickupBacklog?: boolean and requireUatBeforeMerge?: boolean to FlywheelLifecycleOptions in src/lib/cloister/flywheel.ts. Resolve them from app_settings (isFlywheelAutoPickupBacklog/isFlywheelRequireUatBeforeMerge) in spawnFlywheel and resumeFlywheel so the spawned orchestrator sees the live values. In defaultFlywheelPrompt (flywheel.ts:51) extend the configLines array to render: `Auto-pickup backlog: <true|false>` and `Require UAT before merge: <true|false>`. Update resolveFlywheelRoleConfig() in src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts to read and forward the same two values when CLI starts a run.

subItems.title: FlywheelLifecycleOptions interface includes optional autoPickupBacklog and requireUatBeforeMerge booleans.
spawnFlywheel() and resumeFlywheel() resolve undefined values from app_settings via the new getters from b01 before calling spawnFlywheelAgent; no caller has to know the default values.
defaultFlywheelPrompt renders the two lines verbatim (e.g. 'Auto-pickup backlog: false') and the configSection appears in the prompt body when at least one option is set.

Vitest unit tests in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts cover the 2x2 truth table at the brief-rendering level: (false,true), (false,false), (true,true), (true,false) — each expectation asserts the configSection substring exactly.
npm run typecheck and npm test pass; src/cli/commands/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts still passes (no regressions in CLI spawn).

items.title: Update roles/flywheel.md Inventory step to conditionally include backlog when auto_pickup_backlog=true

narrative.Action: Edit the '1. **Inventory** —' bullet in roles/flywheel.md to add a paragraph: when the brief shows 'Auto-pickup backlog: true', also include READY backlog items (no status in progress yet) bounded by maxAgents, and emit 'start' or 'plan' suggestions for them. When false (today's default), inventory is restricted to work in progress / in review / blocked / awaiting merge as today.

subItems.title: Inventory step explicitly references the 'Auto-pickup backlog: <bool>' line in the brief and describes both behaviors (true/false) in a single conditional paragraph.
Backlog-inclusion mode preserves all existing inventory filters (eltmon author/assignee gate; exclude needs-design / needs-discussion) — these are not relaxed when auto_pickup_backlog=true.
Backlog-inclusion mode caps total active agents at maxAgents from the brief; the orchestrator must not exceed it.
No other section of roles/flywheel.md is changed by this bead.

items.title: Update roles/flywheel.md forbidden list to gate merge action on require_uat_before_merge

narrative.Action: Edit the 'Never:' list in roles/flywheel.md to replace the 'Merge PRs directly or auto-merge a PR without human UAT and merge approval' bullet with a conditional: 'Merge PRs directly. (Auto-merge is permitted only when the brief shows Require UAT before merge: false — schedule via POST /api/flywheel/auto-merge/schedule and never invoke gh pr merge yourself.)'. Add a new 'Allowed (when require_uat_before_merge=false):' subsection naming the schedule endpoint.

subItems.title: Forbidden bullet is rewritten as a conditional and references both the brief config line and the schedule endpoint by name.
The orchestrator is still forbidden from invoking gh pr merge directly under any setting; only the schedule endpoint is allowed.
An 'Allowed' subsection names the new schedule endpoint and clarifies that the operator-cancellable cooldown is server-managed.
No other section of roles/flywheel.md is changed by this bead.

items.title: Add GET/POST /api/flywheel/config routes for the two toggles

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts add two new routes: GET /api/flywheel/config returning {auto_pickup_backlog, require_uat_before_merge}; POST /api/flywheel/config accepting {auto_pickup_backlog?, require_uat_before_merge?} and updating only the provided keys (partial update). POST passes through requireTrustedOrigin() and validates each value is boolean. Both keys appear in the response after either call.

subItems.title: GET /api/flywheel/config returns the two toggles and their current values without authentication required (read-only).
POST /api/flywheel/config is origin-validated; missing-origin returns 403 with the same error shape as other flywheel POST routes.
POST with {auto_pickup_backlog: 'yes'} or any non-boolean value returns 400 with a clear error naming the offending key.
POST with a partial body updates only the named key; the other key is untouched.
Vitest route tests in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts cover GET, POST happy path (each key), 403 on missing origin, 400 on invalid body, partial update.

items.title: Add two toggle checkboxes to FlywheelPage header wired to /api/flywheel/config

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx add two checkbox controls in the header rail (near the run-id badge). Implement a useFlywheelConfig + useFlywheelConfigMutation hook pair following the useDeaconPause shape (TanStack Query, 5s staleTime, optimistic onSuccess). Each checkbox shows a short tooltip explaining the behavior. Layout is responsive and does not push the existing freshness/tick metrics off-screen.

subItems.title: Two checkboxes labeled 'Auto-pickup backlog' and 'Require UAT before merge' render in the FlywheelPage header.
Initial values are fetched from /api/flywheel/config; toggling either sends POST /api/flywheel/config with the new partial value and updates the cached query data optimistically.
While the mutation is in flight the checkbox is disabled; on error it reverts and surfaces a small inline error (red text under the checkbox).
Each checkbox has a title= tooltip describing the off/on behavior verbatim (matches the descriptions in the issue body).
Verified manually in a browser via npm run dev: toggling each checkbox round-trips through the server and persists across page reload.
Vitest component test in src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/__tests__/FlywheelPage.test.tsx covers: initial render reads /api/flywheel/config, click sends POST, optimistic update.

items.title: Add `pan flywheel config --get/--set` CLI subcommand + skill update

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts add a 'config' subcommand under the flywheel group with two mutually-exclusive forms: `pan flywheel config --get [key]` (prints both keys when no key, or just the requested key) and `pan flywheel config --set <key>=<bool>` (sets one key). Only the two PAN-1486 keys are accepted; unknown keys exit non-zero with a clear error. Update sync-sources/skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md to document both forms with examples.

subItems.title: `pan flywheel config --get` prints both keys with their current values in `<key>=<bool>` form on stdout, exit 0.
`pan flywheel config --get flywheel.require_uat_before_merge` prints only that key, exit 0.
`pan flywheel config --set flywheel.auto_pickup_backlog=true` sets the value (writes through app_settings), prints the new value, exit 0.
`pan flywheel config --set unknown.key=true` exits non-zero with stderr 'Unknown flywheel config key: unknown.key'.
`pan flywheel config --set flywheel.auto_pickup_backlog=maybe` exits non-zero with stderr explaining boolean is required.
sync-sources/skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md documents both `--get` and `--set` forms with an example for each key; `npm run lint` (which includes scripts/lint-skills.sh) passes.
Vitest tests in src/cli/commands/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts cover get-all, get-one, set-valid, set-unknown-key, set-non-boolean.

narrative.Action: Add a new module src/lib/cloister/auto-merge-eligibility.ts exporting BLOCKER_LABELS = ['needs-design', 'needs-discussion', 'do-not-merge'] and an async isAutoMergeEligible(issueId: string): Promise<{eligible: boolean; reason?: string}>. The predicate combines: (a) read-only review status check for readyForMerge===true; (b) GitHub PR state check via getPullRequestState() with checksFailed===false and merged===false; (c) GitHub issue labels check that none of the issue's labels intersect BLOCKER_LABELS. Returns a reason string when ineligible so callers can use it in TTS / status messages.

subItems.title: BLOCKER_LABELS is exported as a readonly tuple from the new module; the role prompt and any future surface should import this constant rather than hard-coding the strings.
isAutoMergeEligible returns {eligible:false, reason: 'review status is not readyForMerge'} when readyForMerge is false.
Returns {eligible:false, reason: 'CI checks failing on PR HEAD <sha>'} when checksFailed===true.
Returns {eligible:false, reason: 'PR is already merged'} when prState.merged===true.
Returns {eligible:false, reason: 'issue carries blocker label: <label>'} when any BLOCKER_LABELS member is on the issue.
Returns {eligible:true} only when all checks pass.
Vitest unit tests in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/auto-merge-eligibility.test.ts cover all five branches with mocked review-status + getPullRequestState + label-fetch.

items.title: Add pending_auto_merges SQLite table (schema v28) + CRUD module

narrative.Action: Bump src/lib/database/schema.ts CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION from 27 to 28; add a v27→v28 migration block CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_auto_merges (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, issueId TEXT NOT NULL, prUrl TEXT NOT NULL, prNumber INTEGER, projectKey TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK status IN ('pending','merging','blocked','failed'), scheduledMergeAt TEXT NOT NULL, scheduledAt TEXT NOT NULL, mergedAt TEXT, failureReason TEXT, cancelledAt TEXT, cancelledBy TEXT). Add CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_pending_auto_merges_active_issue ON pending_auto_merges(issueId) WHERE status IN ('pending','merging'). Create src/lib/database/pending-auto-merges-db.ts with scheduleAutoMerge, getPendingAutoMerge(issueId), listPendingAutoMerges, transitionToMerging(id), markFailed(id, reason), markBlocked(id, reason), markMerged(id), cancelPending(id, cancelledBy), clearAllPending() — all sync and wrapped in Effect.try with DatabaseError tag like app-settings.ts.

subItems.title: CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION is 28 and a fresh-DB init creates the pending_auto_merges table with the schema described in the Action.
v27→v28 migration block runs on an existing v27 DB without dropping any existing data; the table appears after migration.

UNIQUE partial index prevents two simultaneously-active (pending OR merging) entries for the same issueId; a second scheduleAutoMerge for the same issue returns the existing entry rather than inserting.
transitionToMerging is atomic — uses UPDATE ... WHERE id=? AND status='pending' and returns boolean indicating whether the row transitioned; concurrent calls produce exactly one winner.
cancelPending refuses (returns false / 409-shape) when the entry is in 'merging' state; succeeds when status='pending'.
clearAllPending() removes all entries whose status='pending' but leaves 'merging' / 'failed' / 'blocked' historical entries intact.
scheduledMergeAt and scheduledAt columns are documented (jsdoc on the type) as absolute ISO timestamps that survive process sleep; failureReason free-text up to 1024 chars.

Vitest tests in src/lib/database/__tests__/pending-auto-merges-db.test.ts cover: schedule + read-back; schedule-duplicate-idempotent; transition pending→merging exactly once across concurrent calls; cancelPending honors status; clearAllPending behavior.
Vitest schema test in src/lib/database/__tests__/stuck-schema.test.ts (or sibling) asserts CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION === 28 and that the table is present after migrate-from-v27.

items.title: Add POST /api/flywheel/auto-merge/schedule + scanner that enqueues with cooldown and TTS

narrative.Action: Add POST /api/flywheel/auto-merge/schedule (request body: {issueId}) and GET /api/flywheel/auto-merge/pending (returns the full active list). The schedule handler checks all gates in order: isFlywheelRequireUatBeforeMerge() === false (else 412); isFlywheelGloballyPaused() === false (else 423); resolveLiveFlywheelRunId() !== null (else 412); isAutoMergeEligible(issueId).eligible === true (else 422 with reason). On gate pass: scheduleAutoMerge() with scheduledMergeAt = now + 5 minutes; emit TTS via the same path as scripts/say.sh used by the dashboard ('PAN-XXX auto-merging in 5 minutes; pan merge cancel PAN-XXX to abort'); return the entry. Constant AUTO_MERGE_COOLDOWN_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000 in a new auto-merge-config.ts module.

subItems.title: AUTO_MERGE_COOLDOWN_MS = 300_000 exported from src/lib/cloister/auto-merge-config.ts; not duplicated elsewhere.

POST /api/flywheel/auto-merge/schedule with valid {issueId} when require_uat_before_merge=false, flywheel running, and PR eligible: returns 200 with the persisted entry containing scheduledMergeAt = now + 300s (±2s tolerance for test wall-clock).
POST when require_uat_before_merge=true returns 412 with a body explaining UAT is still required.
POST when flywheel paused returns 423 (Locked) with a body explaining the flywheel is paused.
POST when isAutoMergeEligible returns {eligible:false} returns 422 with the eligibility reason verbatim in the body.
POST is origin-validated; missing/wrong origin returns 403.
TTS announcement fires exactly once per (issueId, 'scheduled') transition; duplicate schedule calls return 200 with the existing entry and do NOT re-announce.
GET /api/flywheel/auto-merge/pending returns the active list sorted by scheduledMergeAt ascending; entries include {id, issueId, scheduledMergeAt, scheduledAt, status, prNumber, prUrl}.
Vitest route tests cover each branch above; TTS announcement is mocked and asserted exactly-once.

items.title: Add server-side executor that fires pending auto-merges after cooldown via the existing merge code path

narrative.Action: Add a small interval-based executor (src/dashboard/server/services/auto-merge-executor.ts) started from main.ts when the dashboard boots. Tick every 30s: listPendingAutoMerges() with status='pending' AND scheduledMergeAt <= now; for each entry: re-check isFlywheelGloballyPaused() (skip if true), re-check isAutoMergeEligible (if no longer eligible mark status='blocked' with reason); then transitionToMerging(id) — if it returns false (lost the race), skip. On winner: invoke the same internal merge function the dashboard MERGE button uses in src/dashboard/server/routes/workspaces.ts; on success markMerged; on throw markFailed with reason. Stop the executor cleanly on dashboard shutdown.

subItems.title: Executor starts when the dashboard server boots and is registered alongside the existing Deacon patrol so dev-mode and prod both pick it up; it can be disabled via env (PANOPTICON_DISABLE_AUTO_MERGE=1) for tests.
Tick processes only entries whose scheduledMergeAt <= Date.now(); future-scheduled entries remain pending.
If isFlywheelGloballyPaused() is true at tick time, the executor logs '[auto-merge] flywheel paused, skipping tick' and does NOT fire any merge; entries remain pending.
If isAutoMergeEligible returns false at fire-time (e.g. CI flipped red during cooldown), the entry is marked status='blocked' with the eligibility reason and the executor moves on without firing.
transitionToMerging is called and only the winning concurrent call proceeds; the loser logs and continues.

Successful merge: status set to 'merging' then 'merged' with mergedAt timestamp; the existing dashboard merge function (the one wired to the MERGE button) is invoked unchanged — no second merge path is added.
Failed merge: status set to 'failed' with failureReason; TTS announces 'PAN-XXX auto-merge failed: <reason>'; entry persists for the orchestrator to surface.

Vitest tests with mocked merge function cover: future-scheduled-skipped, paused-skipped, eligibility-recheck-blocked, transition-race-loser-skips, successful-merge happy path, failed-merge marks failed and announces.
Tests use vi.useFakeTimers() — no real interval delays per the CLAUDE.md retry/delay rule.

items.title: Surface failed/blocked auto-merges as 'investigate' suggestions in FlywheelStatus

narrative.Action: Document in roles/flywheel.md that when assembling each tick's suggestions[], the orchestrator must call GET /api/flywheel/auto-merge/pending, and for every entry with status='failed' or 'blocked', emit a suggestion {action:'investigate', issueId, rationale: 'auto-merge <status>: <failureReason>', priority:'high'}. The entry stays in the table until the operator clears it (cancelling the entry counts as clearing). No code change beyond the role prompt update.

subItems.title: roles/flywheel.md Tick loop step 3 ('Emit suggestions') gains a sub-bullet describing the auto-merge surface call and the suggestion shape; references GET /api/flywheel/auto-merge/pending by name.
Role prompt explicitly says the failed/blocked entry remains in the table until cancelled — the orchestrator does not auto-clear them.

items.title: Add `pan merge cancel <id>` CLI + DELETE /api/flywheel/auto-merge/:id route

narrative.Action: Add a new top-level `merge` command group with one subcommand `cancel <id>` in a new file src/cli/commands/merge.ts. The handler calls DELETE /api/flywheel/auto-merge/:id (also new) which calls cancelPending(); on 200 prints 'Cancelled auto-merge for <id>' and emits TTS 'auto-merge cancelled for <id>'; on 409 (entry in 'merging' state) prints a clear message and exits non-zero; on 404 prints 'No pending auto-merge for <id>' and exits non-zero. Register the new command in src/cli/index.ts. Add a new skill sync-sources/skills/pan-merge/SKILL.md documenting the cancel subcommand.

subItems.title: `pan merge cancel PAN-123` for a pending entry exits 0 and prints the cancellation confirmation; the entry is removed from the active list.
`pan merge cancel PAN-123` when the entry is already 'merging' exits non-zero with a message explaining the cooldown has expired and the merge is in progress.
`pan merge cancel PAN-999` for a non-pending issue exits non-zero with 'No pending auto-merge for PAN-999'.
DELETE /api/flywheel/auto-merge/:id is origin-validated; non-trusted origin returns 403.
DELETE on a 'pending' entry returns 200 with the cancelled entry body; on 'merging' returns 409; on missing returns 404.
TTS announcement 'auto-merge cancelled for <issueId>' fires exactly once per cancellation.
sync-sources/skills/pan-merge/SKILL.md exists, documents `pan merge cancel <id>`, and `npm run lint` passes (skill ↔ CLI cross-check OK).
Vitest tests in src/cli/commands/__tests__/merge.test.ts and src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts cover the happy / merging / missing / origin paths.

items.title: Add pending auto-merges banner with countdown + cancel button to FlywheelPage

narrative.Action: Add a PendingAutoMergesBanner component rendered above the FlywheelPage main split when GET /api/flywheel/auto-merge/pending returns at least one entry with status='pending'. Each row shows: issueId (clickable), PR number, remaining cooldown ('auto-merging in 3:42'), and a 'Cancel' button. Cancel calls DELETE /api/flywheel/auto-merge/:id; on success the row disappears via query invalidation. Countdown updates every second from the entry's absolute scheduledMergeAt timestamp.

subItems.title: Banner only renders when there is at least one entry with status='pending'; empty list renders nothing (no empty placeholder).
Each row's countdown updates every second based on Date.parse(scheduledMergeAt) - Date.now(); when remaining <= 0 the row shows 'merging…' (executor will pick it up).
Cancel button is disabled while the mutation is in flight and while the entry is in 'merging' state.
Cancel mutation invalidates the pending query so the row disappears from all open tabs within the next refetch.
issueId is a link that opens the issue's existing dashboard route.
Verified manually in npm run dev: schedule via curl, observe banner appear, click cancel, banner row disappears.
Vitest component test renders the banner with a fixed scheduledMergeAt, advances fake timers, and asserts cancel triggers the DELETE call.

items.title: End-to-end integration test: cooldown respected, cancel works, executor fires on expiry

narrative.Action: Add src/lib/cloister/__tests__/auto-merge-integration.test.ts that wires the scheduler → executor → cancel together end-to-end with mocked merge function and mocked GitHub PR state. Three scenarios: (1) schedule + wait 4 minutes (fake timers) + cancel → entry removed, merge function never called; (2) schedule + wait full 5 minutes + executor tick → merge function called exactly once, entry status='merged'; (3) schedule + flip CI red during cooldown + executor tick → entry status='blocked', merge function never called.

subItems.title: Test uses vi.useFakeTimers() and vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync — no real-wall-clock delays.
Scenario 1 (cancel during cooldown): cancel returns 200, the pending list becomes empty, the mocked merge function is never invoked even after the cooldown elapses.
Scenario 2 (cooldown expires): the mocked merge function is invoked exactly once, the entry transitions pending→merging→merged.
Scenario 3 (eligibility flips during cooldown): the entry transitions pending→blocked with the eligibility reason; merge function is never invoked.
All three scenarios run in under 100ms wall-clock thanks to fake timers.
npm run typecheck and npm test pass; full suite is green.
